data.output:
Manage beads — portable, ephemeral work items with metadata.

Beads provide a lightweight work queue for AI agents and developers.
They track tasks, dependencies, and status without coupling to any
specific workflow methodology.

Examples:
  ddx bead init                                    # Initialize bead storage
  ddx bead create "Fix auth bug" --type bug        # Create a bead
  ddx bead list --status open                      # List open beads
  ddx bead ready                                   # Show beads ready for work
  ddx bead dep add <id> <dep-id>                   # Add a dependency
  ddx bead import --from jsonl beads.jsonl          # Import from JSONL

Usage:
  ddx bead [flags]
  ddx bead [command]

Aliases:
  bead, beads

Available Commands:
  ac-check              Mechanically verify acceptance criteria against the working tree
  archive               Archive closed beads when active beads.jsonl exceeds size threshold
  blocked               List externally blocked beads
  clear-cooldown        Bulk-clear work cooldowns across the tracker
  close                 Close a bead
  cooldown              Inspect and clear work cooldowns
  create                Create a new bead
  dep                   Manage bead dependencies
  doctor                Detect (and optionally repair) beads.jsonl rows with oversized fields
  evidence              Manage append-only execution evidence
  export                Export beads as JSONL
  human                 Deprecated alias: resolve proposed operator-attention beads
  import                Import beads from external source
  init                  Initialize bead storage
  lint                  Check acceptance criteria mechanical verifiability
  list                  List beads
  merge                 Resolve a Git conflict in .ddx/beads.jsonl
  metrics               Per-bead cost, token, and attempt summaries from execution evidence
  migrate               Split active beads.jsonl into beads + beads-archive + attachments
  needs-human           Deprecated alias: list proposed operator-attention beads
  queue                 Manage in-priority queue ordering
  ready                 List beads ready for work
  reconcile             Reconcile stale bead lifecycle metadata
  reconcile-attachments Commit dirty attachment audit files
  reopen                Reopen a closed bead
  replay                Re-execute a prior attempt with alternate model/harness/prompt
  replay-bench          Run multiple replays in parallel against the same source attempt
  review                Generate a review prompt for a bead's implementation

Create a new bead

Usage:
  ddx bead create <title> [flags]

Flags:
      --acceptance string    Acceptance criteria
      --description string   Description
  -h, --help                 help for create
      --labels string        Comma-separated labels
      --parent string        Parent bead ID
      --priority int         Priority (0=highest, 4=lowest) (default 2)
      --set stringArray      Set custom field (key=value, repeatable)
      --type string          Bead type (task, bug, epic, chore)

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

Manage bead dependencies

Usage:
  ddx bead dep [flags]
  ddx bead dep [command]

Available Commands:
  add         Add a dependency (id depends on dep-id)
  remove      Remove a dependency
  tree        Show dependency tree

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for dep

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

Use "ddx bead dep [command] --help" for more information about a command.
Cancel received, shutting down gracefully

Add a dependency (id depends on dep-id)

Usage:
  ddx bead dep add <id> <dep-id> [flags]

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for add

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

data.text: This bead is 9567 bytes with 7 ACs spanning a new ~bash runner~, a Docker image, 9 shell adapters, Go changes, deactivation, and parity-fixture capture — far past the size cutoff. The instructions explicitly direct a split-first pass. I'll decompose into focused children with dependency edges, then write the rationale.

input.command:
ddx bead create "bench-pr-A1: harbor-runner Docker image + sha-labeled build script" \
  --type task --priority 2 \
  --labels "area:bench, kind:feature, governing:plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification, pr:A" \
  --parent fizeau-11e9c095 \
  --description $'Create the fizeau-harbor-runner Docker image that wraps Harbor and its Python adapters so the host stops needing Python.

FILES:
  - scripts/benchmark/Dockerfile.harbor-runner: FROM python:3.12-slim; pip install harbor-bench==<pinned>; COPY scripts/benchmark/harbor_adapters/ /app/harbor_adapters/; COPY scripts/benchmark/harbor_agent.py /app/harbor_agent.py; ENV PYTHONPATH=/app; WORKDIR /app; ENTRYPOINT ["harbor"].
  - scripts/benchmark/build-harbor-runner.sh: docker build with image-content-sha label = sha256 of harbor_adapters/ + harbor_agent.py + Dockerfile.harbor-runner.

SPIKE (do first, ~5 min): confirm `pip install harbor-bench==<pinned>` resolves; if Harbor is git-only, swap to `pip install git+https://github.com/laude-institute/...`. Record the resolved pin in a comment in the Dockerfile.

OUT OF SCOPE: the ./benchmark preflight subcommand that calls build-harbor-runner.sh (covered by child A3).

ACCEPTANCE:
  1. scripts/benchmark/build-harbor-runner.sh builds successfully and emits an image tagged fizeau-harbor-runner:latest with a non-empty image-content-sha label.
  2. docker run --rm fizeau-harbor-runner:latest --help exits 0 and prints Harbor CLI help.
  3. Re-running build-harbor-runner.sh without source changes reuses the cached image (label sha unchanged).' \
  --acceptance 'All 3 commands in the description ACCEPTANCE block pass.' 2>&1
input.description: Create child A1: harbor-runner Docker image

input.command:
ddx bead create "bench-pr-A2: shell harness-adapters + harbor task-executor + CONTRACT.md + runtime-probe.sh" \
  --type task --priority 2 \
  --labels "area:bench, kind:feature, governing:plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification, pr:A" \
  --parent fizeau-11e9c095 \
  --description $'Port the matrix-side Python harness adapters and Harbor task executor to shell, plus the runtime probe and CONTRACT.md docs.

REFERENCE: scripts/benchmark/harness_adapters/{fiz,opencode,pi,cost_probe,noop,dumb_script}.py — these are the source of truth for what each adapter emits. cmd/bench/matrix.go:1015-1025 shows how claude/codex are invoked today (--agent-import-path).

FILES:
  - scripts/benchmark/harness-adapters/{fiz,claude,codex,opencode,pi,cost-probe,noop,dumb-script}: 8 executable bash scripts. Each supports:
      <name> install <agent-artifact-path> -> install-spec.json on stdout {install_command, artifact_source, binary_path, harbor_plugin}
      <name> command (reads profile JSON on stdin) -> command-spec.json on stdout {command, env, secret_env_keys}
      Each file starts with a "# SUMMARY:" header line.
  - scripts/benchmark/task-executors/harbor: bash wrapper around `docker run --rm -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock -v $cell_dir:/output -v $tasks_dir:/tasks:ro -e FIZEAU_API_KEY -e FIZEAU_BASE_URL -e FIZEAU_MODEL ... fizeau-harbor-runner:latest run --agent-import-path "$harbor_plugin" --task-id "$task_id" --output /output ...`. Reads task-spec.json from stdin (per CONTRACT.md); writes cell_dir/result.json.
  - scripts/benchmark/harness-adapters/CONTRACT.md and scripts/benchmark/task-executors/CONTRACT.md: document JSON shapes and the "# SUMMARY:" header convention.
  - scripts/benchmark/runtime-probe.sh: case-on profile.metadata.runtime; curl + jq against each runtime's HTTP endpoint; emit model_server JSON {name, version, commit, endpoint}. Backends: lucebox, llamacpp, vllm, omlx, ds4, rapid-mlx (one case arm each).

DEPENDS ON A1 (harbor-runner image must exist so the task-executor can invoke it).

ACCEPTANCE:
  1. Each of the 8 harness-adapters supports `install` and `command` subcommands; `command` reads stdin profile JSON and emits valid JSON with {command, env, secret_env_keys}.
  2. task-executors/harbor accepts task-spec.json on stdin and invokes `docker run ... fizeau-harbor-runner:latest run --agent-import-path ... --task-id ... --output /output ...`.
  3. runtime-probe.sh handles all 6 backends (lucebox, llamacpp, vllm, omlx, ds4, rapid-mlx).
  4. harness-adapters/CONTRACT.md and task-executors/CONTRACT.md document the JSON shapes.
  5. shellcheck passes on all new shell files.' \
  --acceptance 'All 5 commands in the description ACCEPTANCE block pass.' 2>&1
input.description: Create child A2: shell adapters + contracts

input.command:
ddx bead create "bench-pr-A3: bash runner skeleton + listing subcommands + --plan mode" \
  --type task --priority 2 \
  --labels "area:bench, kind:feature, governing:plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification, pr:A" \
  --parent fizeau-11e9c095 \
  --description $'Create scripts/benchmark/benchmark (no extension, executable bash) with arg parsing, listing subcommands, profile/bench-set/concurrency-group YAML reading, matrix expansion, preflight that triggers harbor-runner rebuild, and --plan output. No execution loop in this bead (covered by A4).

FILES:
  - scripts/benchmark/benchmark (new bash script).

DISPATCH:
  ./benchmark <subcommand> ... with subcommands: profiles | bench-sets | task-executors | harness-adapters | validate | preflight | (no subcommand = run mode).

RUN-MODE FLAGS (only --plan is required for this bead):
  --profile P[,P2,...] --bench-set B[,B2,...] [--plan] [--out DIR]

MATRIX EXPANSION:
  - Read profile/bench-set/concurrency-group YAML with `yq -o=json`.
  - profile × task × rep -> ordered cell list. Resolve `tasks_from:` (path relative to bench-set file) AND inline `tasks:`.
  - PR C will finalize the bench-set schema; for this bead default `task_executor` to `harbor` when `framework == terminal-bench`.

PREFLIGHT:
  - `./benchmark preflight` invokes scripts/benchmark/build-harbor-runner.sh if cached image label sha differs from current source sha.

PLAN OUTPUT:
  - `--plan` prints the matrix (one line per cell) to stdout and exits 0; creates NO files; builds NO Docker images.

DEPENDS ON A1 (preflight references the image build) and A2 (listing subcommands enumerate harness-adapters/ and task-executors/).

ACCEPTANCE:
  1. ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark profiles, bench-sets, harness-adapters, task-executors each list available entries.
  2. ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark --plan --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary prints the matrix, creates NO files, builds NO Docker images.
  3. ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark preflight rebuilds harbor-runner only when sha differs.
  4. shellcheck passes on the new script.' \
  --acceptance 'AC2 satisfies AC1 of the parent bead. All 4 commands in the description ACCEPTANCE block pass.' 2>&1
input.description: Create child A3: bash runner skeleton + --plan

input.command:
ddx bead create "bench-pr-A4: bash runner execution loop + harbor invocation + report.json + sweep.json + per-cell retry" \
  --type task --priority 2 \
  --labels "area:bench, kind:feature, governing:plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification, pr:A" \
  --parent fizeau-11e9c095 \
  --description $'Add the per-cell execution loop, resume default, harbor invocation glue, report.json composition, sweep.json metadata capture, and per-cell bounded retry to scripts/benchmark/benchmark. Concurrency/signal handling lives in A5; budget enforcement lives in A6.

FLAGS ADDED in this bead: [--force-rerun] [--retry-invalid] [--reps N]

RESUME DEFAULT:
  - If cell_dir/report.json exists with terminal final_status -> skip.
  - --force-rerun ignores.
  - --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class OR orphan cell-state.json.

PER-CELL LOOP:
  - Write cell-state.json sentinel.
  - Invoke harness-adapters/<name> command < profile.json -> command-spec.json.
  - Compose task-spec.
  - Invoke task-executors/<name> < task-spec.json (writes cell_dir/result.json).
  - Compose report.json (embed profile, command, env_redacted, fiz.txt, fiz.err, session/).
  - Delete cell-state.json on terminal close.

SWEEP.JSON:
  - Capture task_executor_version + harbor_runner_image_digest once per ./benchmark invocation into <out>/sweep.json; runner copies into each cell.

PER-CELL RETRY:
  - Bounded exponential backoff for transient errors (connection refused, 5xx, EOF parse).
  - Write attempt_of: link in the new cell; back-write superseded_by: into the prior cell (the one allowed mutation of a closed cell).

DEPENDS ON A3 (skeleton + matrix expansion), A1, A2.

ACCEPTANCE:
  1. ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary produces cells under bench/results/fiz-tools-v1/cells/, each with report.json containing embedded profile, command, env_redacted, fiz.txt, fiz.err, session/.
  2. <out>/sweep.json contains task_executor_version and harbor_runner_image_digest; each cell's report.json references them.
  3. Re-running without --force-rerun skips cells with terminal report.json.
  4. --retry-invalid reruns cells with invalid_class or orphan cell-state.json; new cell has attempt_of link, prior cell has superseded_by back-link.
  5. Inducing a transient 5xx during a cell triggers bounded exponential backoff and eventual success (or terminal failure after retry cap).' \
  --acceptance 'AC1 satisfies AC2 of the parent bead. All 5 commands in the description ACCEPTANCE block pass.' 2>&1
input.description: Create child A4: execution loop + retry

input.command:
ddx bead create "bench-pr-A5: bash runner concurrency-group flock + SIGTERM/SIGINT handling + in-flight.json" \
  --type task --priority 2 \
  --labels "area:bench, kind:feature, governing:plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification, pr:A" \
  --parent fizeau-11e9c095 \
  --description $'Add concurrency-group locking, --jobs background pool, signal handling, and the concurrent-cells state file to scripts/benchmark/benchmark.

FLAGS ADDED: [--jobs N]

CONCURRENCY:
  - flock per concurrency-group at ${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR:-${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/fizeau-benchmark}/locks/<group>.lock.
  - setsid for each cell process group.
  - Background-job pool capped by --jobs.

SIGNAL HANDLING:
  - Trap SIGINT/SIGTERM. Stop accepting new cells.
  - SIGTERM each in-flight cell's process group AND `docker stop` each in-flight harbor-runner container.
  - Wait 30s, then SIGKILL.
  - Write final_status=interrupted to in-flight cells (process_outcome=killed, no metrics).
  - Exit non-zero.

IN-FLIGHT STATE:
  - ${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}/<hostname>/in-flight.json.
  - Runner appends its cell at start under flock, reads current count, writes into cell, removes at close.
  - Prune stale entries (PID dead) at next read.

DEPENDS ON A4 (execution loop must exist to interrupt).

ACCEPTANCE:
  1. SIGTERM to a mid-sweep ./benchmark: in-flight cells marked final_status=interrupted, harbor-runner containers stopped via `docker stop`, runner exits non-zero. Verify: ./benchmark --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary & sleep 5; kill -TERM $! ; wait; jq .final_status bench/results/.../report.json prints "interrupted".
  2. Two concurrent ./benchmark invocations with the same concurrency-group serialize via flock; no overlapping cells.
  3. in-flight.json reflects the current cell count; stale PID entries pruned on next read.' \
  --acceptance 'AC1 satisfies AC5 of the parent bead. All 3 commands in the description ACCEPTANCE block pass.' 2>&1

ddx bead create "bench-pr-A6: bash runner budget enforcement + budget.json + budget_halted placeholder" \
  --type task --priority 2 \
  --labels "area:bench, kind:feature, governing:plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification, pr:A" \
  --parent fizeau-11e9c095 \
  --description $'Add per-cell cost accounting and a halt-before-next-cell budget cap to scripts/benchmark/benchmark.

FLAGS ADDED: [--max-cost-usd X]

BEHAVIOR:
  - Compute cost_usd_at_run_time per cell from profile.pricing × token streams at cell close.
  - Accumulate into <out>/budget.json.
  - When the cap is hit, halt before the next cell.
  - Skipped cells get final_status=budget_halted placeholder with process_outcome=setup_failed and a `note` field describing the cap.

DEPENDS ON A4 (cell close path produces the token streams).

ACCEPTANCE:
  1. ./scripts/benchmark/benchmark --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary --max-cost-usd 0.01 produces at least one cell with final_status=budget_halted.
  2. <out>/budget.json accumulates cost_usd_at_run_time across closed cells.
  3. Budget-halted cells have process_outcome=setup_failed and a non-empty `note` field referencing the cap.' \
  --acceptance 'AC1 satisfies AC3 of the parent bead. All 3 commands in the description ACCEPTANCE block pass.' 2>&1

ddx bead create "bench-pr-A7: agent-leak fix in execute_native.go + sampling.planning_mode field + profile migration" \
  --type task --priority 2 \
  --labels "area:bench, kind:fix, governing:plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification, pr:A" \
  --parent fizeau-11e9c095 \
  --description $'Remove the implicit benchmark-preset planning-mode coupling in the Go service, expose planning_mode in the profile schema, and migrate any profile that depended on the old behavior.

GO CHANGE:
  - internal/serviceimpl/execute_native.go:265 currently reads:
      PlanningMode: req.PlanningMode || req.ToolPreset == "benchmark",
    Delete the `|| req.ToolPreset == "benchmark"` clause.

SCHEMA + ADAPTER:
  - Add field `sampling.planning_mode: bool` (default false) to the profile YAML schema.
  - harness-adapters/fiz reads it and adds `--plan` to the command when true.

PROFILE MIGRATION:
  - Spike: `rg -l 'preset.*benchmark|FIZEAU_PRESET' scripts/benchmark/profiles/` to find profiles relying on the old preset coupling.
  - Add `sampling.planning_mode: true` to each.

DEPENDS ON A2 (harness-adapters/fiz must exist to consume the new field).

ACCEPTANCE:
  1. `grep -n 'ToolPreset == "benchmark"' internal/serviceimpl/execute_native.go` returns nothing.
  2. Profile YAML schema documents `sampling.planning_mode: bool` (default false).
  3. harness-adapters/fiz emits `--plan` in the command when the profile sets sampling.planning_mode=true.
  4. Every profile previously relying on the implicit benchmark preset for planning mode has sampling.planning_mode: true.' \
  --acceptance 'AC1 satisfies AC7 of the parent bead. All 4 commands in the description ACCEPTANCE block pass.' 2>&1
input.description: Create child A7: agent-leak fix + planning_mode

input.command:
ddx bead create "bench-pr-A8: parity fixture capture (go-runner + bash-runner) + diff.sh" \
  --type task --priority 2 \
  --labels "area:bench, kind:test, governing:plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification, pr:A" \
  --parent fizeau-11e9c095 \
  --description $'Capture canary fixtures from both the Go runner and the new bash runner so deactivation (A9) can land safely, and ship a bash+jq diff tool that gates field drift against an allowlist.

ORDER:
  1. First (BEFORE A9 deactivation): run the Go runner against canary (sindri-lucebox + tb-2-1-canary, 3 reps). Commit 3 representative cell reports to scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/go-runner/.
  2. Then run the bash runner against the same canary. Commit cells to scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/bash-runner/.

TOOL:
  - scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/diff.sh: bash + jq tool that reports field divergence outside an allowlist (cell-id, timestamps, paths, NEW fields like harbor_runner_image_digest, model_server.commit, etc.).

DEPENDS ON A4 (bash runner must produce report.json), A5 (clean termination so canary completes), A6 (so budget cap doesn't corrupt fixtures).

ACCEPTANCE:
  1. scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/go-runner/ contains 3 representative cell reports from the existing Go runner.
  2. scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/bash-runner/ contains the matching cells from the bash runner.
  3. scripts/benchmark/testdata/parity/diff.sh against the committed go-runner fixtures is clean per allowlist.
  4. shellcheck passes on diff.sh.' \
  --acceptance 'AC3 satisfies AC4 of the parent bead. All 4 commands in the description ACCEPTANCE block pass.' 2>&1
input.description: Create child A8: parity fixtures + diff

input.command:
ddx bead create "bench-pr-A9: deactivate Go runner execution subcommands with redirect" \
  --type task --priority 2 \
  --labels "area:bench, kind:chore, governing:plan-2026-05-15-benchmark-runner-simplification, pr:A" \
  --parent fizeau-11e9c095 \
  --description $'Short-circuit fiz-bench execution subcommands (matrix, sweep, run, plan) so users land on the new bash runner. Listing subcommands (profiles, bench-sets) stay functional until PR F.

GO CHANGE:
  - cmd/bench/main.go: each execution subcommand (matrix, sweep, run, plan) prints a one-line redirect "use ./benchmark — see scripts/benchmark/README.md" to stderr and exits 2.

DEPENDS ON A4 (bash runner usable) and A8 (parity fixtures captured FIRST per the parent's "must happen BEFORE deactivation merges" note).

ACCEPTANCE:
  1. `fiz-bench matrix 2>&1 | grep -q 'use ./benchmark'; test $? -eq 0`.
  2. `fiz-bench sweep`, `fiz-bench run`, `fiz-bench plan` each exit 2 with the same redirect message.
  3. `fiz-bench profiles` and `fiz-bench bench-sets` still function (preserved listing subcommands).' \
  --acceptance 'AC1 satisfies AC6 of the parent bead. All 3 commands in the description ACCEPTANCE block pass.' 2>&1
input.description: Create child A9: Go runner deactivation

data.text: Now I'll wire up the dependency edges so A3 depends on A1+A2, A4 depends on A3, A5/A6/A8 depend on A4, A7 depends on A2, A9 depends on A4+A8.

input.command:
set -e
ddx bead dep add fizeau-fe87a3ac fizeau-4cb07023   # A3 ← A1
ddx bead dep add fizeau-fe87a3ac fizeau-53a360a1   # A3 ← A2
ddx bead dep add fizeau-a2fd070b fizeau-fe87a3ac   # A4 ← A3
ddx bead dep add fizeau-86f89221 fizeau-a2fd070b   # A5 ← A4
ddx bead dep add fizeau-25209a13 fizeau-a2fd070b   # A6 ← A4
ddx bead dep add fizeau-4cb31dc5 fizeau-a2fd070b   # A8 ← A4
ddx bead dep add fizeau-4cb31dc5 fizeau-86f89221   # A8 ← A5
ddx bead dep add fizeau-4cb31dc5 fizeau-25209a13   # A8 ← A6
ddx bead dep add fizeau-9c53ac4e fizeau-53a360a1   # A7 ← A2
ddx bead dep add fizeau-f7c4e686 fizeau-a2fd070b   # A9 ← A4
ddx bead dep add fizeau-f7c4e686 fizeau-4cb31dc5   # A9 ← A8
echo "deps OK"

ddx bead update fizeau-11e9c095 --notes 'decomposed into fizeau-4cb07023 (A1: harbor-runner image), fizeau-53a360a1 (A2: shell adapters + CONTRACT.md + runtime-probe), fizeau-fe87a3ac (A3: bash runner skeleton + --plan, satisfies parent AC1), fizeau-a2fd070b (A4: execution loop + retry, satisfies parent AC2), fizeau-86f89221 (A5: concurrency + signals, satisfies parent AC5), fizeau-25209a13 (A6: budget, satisfies parent AC3), fizeau-9c53ac4e (A7: agent-leak fix + planning_mode, satisfies parent AC7), fizeau-4cb31dc5 (A8: parity fixtures + diff.sh, satisfies parent AC4), fizeau-f7c4e686 (A9: Go runner deactivation, satisfies parent AC6). Deps: A3<-A1,A2; A4<-A3; A5,A6,A8<-A4; A8<-A5,A6; A7<-A2; A9<-A4,A8. Parent bead description was 9567 bytes (>8000) covering bash runner core + Docker image + 9 shell adapters + Go changes + deactivation + parity capture — split-first per execute-bead Step 0.' 2>&1

input.content:
status: open
orchestrator_action: decompose
reason: Bead description was 9567 bytes (>8000-byte split threshold) with 7 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems — a new bash runner (arg parsing, matrix expansion, execution loop, concurrency/signals, budget, per-cell retry), a Docker image (Dockerfile + sha-labeled build), 9 shell adapters + 2 CONTRACT.md docs + runtime-probe, a Go agent-leak fix + new profile schema field, a Go runner deactivation, and a parity-fixture capture step. Far past the size cutoff for a single bead. Per execute-bead Step 0, decomposition is the success criterion; implementation is left to the child beads.

Children (created with --parent fizeau-11e9c095 and inter-child dep edges):
  - fizeau-4cb07023  A1: harbor-runner Docker image + build-harbor-runner.sh
  - fizeau-53a360a1  A2: shell harness-adapters (8) + task-executors/harbor + CONTRACT.md x2 + runtime-probe.sh
  - fizeau-fe87a3ac  A3: bash runner skeleton + listing subcommands + --plan mode  [parent AC1]
  - fizeau-a2fd070b  A4: execution loop + harbor invocation + report.json + sweep.json + per-cell retry  [parent AC2]
  - fizeau-86f89221  A5: concurrency-group flock + SIGTERM/SIGINT + in-flight.json  [parent AC5]
  - fizeau-25209a13  A6: budget enforcement + budget.json + budget_halted placeholder  [parent AC3]
  - fizeau-9c53ac4e  A7: agent-leak fix in execute_native.go + sampling.planning_mode field + profile migration  [parent AC7]
  - fizeau-4cb31dc5  A8: parity fixture capture (go-runner + bash-runner) + diff.sh  [parent AC4]
  - fizeau-f7c4e686  A9: deactivate fiz-bench execution subcommands with redirect  [parent AC6]

Dependency graph:
  A3 <- A1, A2
  A4 <- A3
  A5, A6, A8 <- A4
  A8 <- A5, A6
  A7 <- A2
  A9 <- A4, A8   (parity fixtures must be captured before Go runner is deactivated)

data.output: File created successfully at: /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-fizeau-11e9c095-20260517T020117-8321163f/.ddx/executions/20260517T020117-8321163f/no_changes_rationale.txt (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
